description_long: This object is an antique flintlock pistol, featuring a wooden stock with a rich, brown patina and a curved grip that fits comfortably in the hand. The barrel is relatively short and made of metal, with a smooth, slightly worn finish, and the muzzle is round and unadorned. The lock mechanism is prominently visible, including the hammer, frizzen, and pan, all crafted from metal and showing signs of age and use. The trigger guard is metallic, rounded, and slightly elongated, providing protection for the trigger while allowing easy access. The overall design is compact and ergonomic, with the wood and metal components seamlessly integrated. This type of firearm would have been used historically for personal defense or dueling, and its craftsmanship suggests it is both functional and decorative.
description: An antique flintlock pistol with a curved wooden grip, short metal barrel, and prominent lock mechanism, blending wood and metal in a compact, historic design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden stock, curved grip, and the entire lock mechanism.
description_view_1: Angled rear view highlighting the rounded grip, trigger guard, and the side profile of the lock mechanism.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the smooth barrel, wooden stock, and the curvature of the grip.
description_view_3: Front-angled view focusing on the muzzle, trigger guard, and the detailed lock mechanism.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, smooth and round with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded and elongated, medium-sized, with a smooth contour and no finger grooves
